test.details: All 13 packages passed: auth, events, gateway, provider, proxy, runtime, sandbox, server, store, types, vmctl, vmmanager

lint.details: No lint script in package.json - not required for validation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update schema documentation to reflect email-based auth: change 'username (TEXT UNIQUE)' to 'email (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL)' in users table description
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: auth-migrate-username-to-email feature changed the schema but library file still references username. auth-storage.md line describes 'users — id (TEXT PK), username (TEXT UNIQUE)'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update Auth HTTP Handlers documentation to reference 'email' instead of 'username' in event payloads: change '{ username, type }' to '{ email, type }'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: frontend-auth-email-input review noted AGENTS.md still references 'username' in frontend-auth-entry documentation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that lint step is optional - projects may not have pnpm run lint configured. Worker should check package.json for script availability.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: frontend-auth-email-input review: skill mentions 'pnpm run lint' but package.json has no lint script. Worker handled gracefully but skill should document this.

nonBlockingIssues.description: JSON marshal/unmarshal errors silently ignored in credential flags handling (webauthn_user.go:53,105). Safe defaults applied but could mask data corruption.
Schema migration for email column appears in migration array but seems like preparation for later feature (auth-migrate-username-to-email). Works correctly but could be confusing.

Schema migration has potentially redundant ALTER TABLE statement. Fresh DBs get proper constraints from schemaDDL; migrated DBs may not retroactively get UNIQUE/NOT NULL constraints. Test coverage confirms it works.
Client-side email validation uses simple regex that may reject some valid internationalized emails. Server-side validation is authoritative using Go's mail.ParseAddress.
Sign count update failure logs at 'warning' level but this could indicate replay attack - consider making more prominent.

summary: All 5 auth-repair features passed scrutiny validation with no blocking issues. Validators (test, vet) all passed. The milestone is ready for user testing. Minor non-blocking issues identified around error handling and documentation consistency that should be addressed in future iterations.
